seoTitle: Webflow Tutorial for Beginners: Your First Page
metaDescription: A beginner's walkthrough of building your first Webflow page, covering sections, containers, classes, spacing, and Flexbox basics.
intro: Getting started with Webflow can feel intimidating if you're new to visual web design, but the core concepts map cleanly to standard HTML and CSS. This beginner tutorial walks through building your first page from scratch — structuring sections and containers, applying reusable classes, and adding basic layout controls.

steps.heading: Explore the Designer workspace
steps.body: Log in, start a blank project, and get familiar with the Elements panel, Style panel, and the left-hand Navigator.

steps.heading: Build the structural hierarchy
steps.body: Drag a Section onto the canvas, then nest a Container inside it to enforce clean, responsive margins.

steps.heading: Add heading and paragraph content
steps.body: Drop a Heading block and a Paragraph text element inside your layout frame.

steps.heading: Create a reusable style class
steps.body: Name a custom class like Hero Title Master, then adjust font size, weight, and line height, along with padding and margin values.

steps.heading: Add layout, images, and a button
steps.body: Use a Div Block with Flexbox to align elements side by side, upload an image from your Assets tray, add a Link Block for a call to action, then preview across mobile and publish.

faq.question: Do I need to know CSS to use Webflow?
faq.answer: No, Webflow's visual style panel maps directly to CSS properties, so you can build without writing code, though understanding CSS concepts helps.

faq.question: What's the difference between a Section and a Container?
faq.answer: A Section is a full-width wrapper, while a Container inside it constrains content to a readable width with consistent side margins.

faq.question: Why should I use classes instead of styling elements individually?
faq.answer: Classes let you reuse the same styling across multiple elements, keeping your design consistent and easier to update later.

faq.question: How do I make my layout responsive?
faq.answer: Use Flexbox alignment tools and preview your page across the built-in mobile and tablet breakpoints before publishing.
